description: Create a content slide on robot training and data collection with a three-column layout: explanatory text on the left and center, plus a right-side section with two line charts showing Open X-Embodiment dataset growth.

header.kicker: Technology Vision 2025 | AI: A Declaration of Autonomy
header.headline: Robot Training / Data Collection

left.title: What is it? / How does it connect to the trend?

items.sub: Part of the power of foundation models today is the robust training data used in their creation. But where LLMs leveraged all written language across the internet, robotics training sets have been scarce. Now, that’s starting to change. New robotics datasets, and the ability to leverage other data types in combination with simulation and real-world reinforcement learning approaches, are helping to fill the gap.

items.text: How does it connect to the trend?
items.sub: If companies want robots to work in different environments and accomplish a wide variety of tasks, their training data must reflect that variety. Assembling initial training sets will be important, but equally important is establishing a framework for ongoing data collection to power subsequent training and learning. Some companies are deploying robot fleets to gather their own real-world training data. Others are leaning into synthetic data, especially for training in simulations where robots can confront nearly limitless scenarios.

center.title: Who is doing it today?
center.body: Many groups have started creating open robotics and embodiment training sets, hoping to accelerate AI for robotics much like how ImageNet helped accelerate computer vision advancement. One example is the Open X-Embodiment Dataset, which is a compilation of 60 open robotic datasets. As for ongoing data collection for training robots, one example comes from the auto industry. Tesla sources training data from its global fleet of cars to improve its Autopilot and Full Self-Driving features, selectively triggering to collect only the most relevant data.

items.text: Open X-Embodiment is an ambitious project to consolidate open robotics datasets into a centralized collection for the training of generalist robot policies.
items.sub: The dataset consists of data from 21 institutions and 22 different robots completing 527 skills, highlighting the growing number of organizations participating in creating open robotics datasets and a large increase in the volume of training data in recent years.
